gate_question: Does this exact current protocol, with current live state, allow an unprivileged attacker to extract funds or rewards beyond entitlement?

candidate.title_or_claim: Missed-interval accumulation in _calculateIntervalsTitanXBurn allows first caller after dormant period to extract amplified TitanX incentive
candidate.claimed_root_cause: amountAllocated for a single interval is set to amountPerInterval * (1 + missedIntervals), capped only at totalTitanXBurn; the 1.5% incentive is then computed on this amplified allocation rather than on a single interval's share
candidate.claimed_impact: Attacker extracts up to 1.5% * totalTitanXBurn (~5.85 billion TitanX at cap) in a single call; currently ~144 missed intervals yield 145x single-interval incentive (~117 trillion raw TitanX units) without any waiting

live_preconditions.name: missedIntervals_at_capture
live_preconditions.source: derived from live_context captured_at 2026-07-14T09:58:29Z and lastBurnedTitanXIntervalStartTimestamp

source_code_basis.why_it_matters: additionalAmount = amountPerInterval * missedIntervals accumulates all missed intervals into a single allocation; the only guard is a cap at totalTitanXBurn, not a per-interval bound
incentive = (currInterval.amountAllocated * INCENTIVE_FEE) / BPS_DENOM is computed on the full amplified allocation and transferred to msg.sender
IntervalAlreadyBurned check only prevents replay within the same interval slot; it does not limit accumulation across missed slots

INCENTIVE_FEE=150, BPS_DENOM=10000, INTERVALS_PER_DAY=144, INTERVAL_TIME=10 minutes confirm the arithmetic: cap triggers at missedIntervals >= 7199 (~50 days); currently ~143 missed intervals yield 144x amplification

call_sequence: 1. Observe lastBurnedTitanXIntervalStartTimestamp and wait until block.timestamp - lastBurnedTitanXIntervalStartTimestamp > N * INTERVAL_TIME for desired N (N=1 already yields 2x; N=143 already elapsed at capture time; N=7199 hits full cap after ~50 days total inactivity)
state_after: attacker receives incentive = amountAllocated * 150 / 10000 in TitanX

extraction_analysis.asset_or_reward: TitanX ERC-20 tokens transferred from contract balance to msg.sender
extraction_analysis.victim_or_protocol_loss: Protocol's totalTitanXBurn reserve is depleted by amountAllocated (up to 100% at cap); future interval callers receive nothing; protocol burn activity is permanently impaired
extraction_analysis.why_gain_exceeds_entitlement: A single caller is entitled to the incentive on one interval's allocation (amountPerInterval * 150 / 10000). The accumulation pattern grants them the incentive on N+1 intervals' worth of allocation in a single call. At current live state this is 144x entitlement; at cap it is 7200x. No mechanism bounds the per-call incentive to a single interval's share.

setup: Instantiate TorusBuyAndProcess at its deployed address
Record attacker TitanX balance before call
transaction_sequence: vm.warp(block.timestamp + 0) // already ~144 missed intervals at capture; optionally vm.warp(block.timestamp + 50 days) for cap test
expected_assertions: assertGt(incentiveReceived, singleIntervalIncentive) // at minimum 2x with any missed interval
// For current live state (~143 missed intervals): assertApproxEqRel(incentiveReceived, singleIntervalIncentive * 144, 1e16)
// For cap test (50 days warp): assertApproxEqRel(incentiveReceived, totalTitanXBurnBefore * 150 / 10000, 1e16)
local_proof_required.reject_if_assertion_fails: If incentiveReceived equals singleIntervalIncentive regardless of elapsed time, the accumulation is bounded and the finding is rejected. If incentiveReceived is proportional to missedIntervals and exceeds singleIntervalIncentive by the expected multiplier, the finding is confirmed.
